factory.crossbow.description: "Had I missed the apple, the second arrow was for you.", Friedrich Schiller, William Tell
factory.crossbow.manager.description: Alpine marksman. His guild drills until the bolt hits apple or tyrant, whichever pays better.
factory.crossbow.manager.name: William Tell
factory.crossbow.name: Crossbow
factory.crossbow.upgrade.description: Counterweight engines and siege crews. Walls crumble, ransoms roll in.
factory.crossbow.upgrade.name: Trebuchets
factory.grain.description: "In the eyes of the hungry there is a growing wrath.", John Steinbeck, The Grapes of Wrath
factory.grain.manager.description: Greek goddess of the threshing floor. Every sheaf in Eleusis feeds both law and lance.
factory.grain.manager.name: Demeter
factory.grain.name: Grain
factory.grain.upgrade.description: Channels and royal silos tied to the river. Famine becomes somebody else's problem.
factory.grain.upgrade.name: Irrigation
factory.iron.description: "He set the great anvil in place and took the hammer in his right hand.", Homer, The Iliad
factory.iron.manager.description: Eternal smith of the Kalevala. His forge never cools; every ingot rings for plough and blade.
factory.iron.manager.name: Ilmarinen
factory.iron.name: Iron
factory.iron.upgrade.description: Turn ingots into blades, mail, buckles, and ploughshares. Arm the levy, shoe the fields.
factory.iron.upgrade.name: Armories
factory.longship.description: "I take to the sea to drive off the spleen.", Herman Melville, Moby-Dick
factory.longship.manager.description: Sailor of Basra and the seven seas. Every hull widens the crown on the water.
factory.longship.manager.name: Sinbad
factory.longship.name: Longship
factory.longship.upgrade.description: Chartered companies, colonies, spice, wool, silver. Every voyage fattens the crown's purse.
factory.longship.upgrade.name: Colonial Trade
factory.reliquary.description: "And then Sir Launcelot saw the Holy Grail covered with white samite.", Thomas Malory, Le Morte d'Arthur
factory.reliquary.manager.description: Martyr of Canterbury. Pilgrims flock with wax-sealed gifts and very sincere awe.
factory.reliquary.manager.name: Thomas Becket
factory.reliquary.name: Reliquary
factory.reliquary.upgrade.description: Every hearth pays tithe, every child learns the creed. Faith becomes coin, by law, not luck.
factory.reliquary.upgrade.name: Tithes
factory.wine.description: "Good wine is a good familiar creature, if it be well used.", William Shakespeare, Othello
factory.wine.manager.description: Keeper of the vine by the sea. Her tavern turns grape into liquid gold faster than any vine alone.
factory.wine.manager.name: Siduri
factory.wine.name: Wine
factory.wine.upgrade.description: Taverns at every crossroad and harbor. Pilgrims, merchants, and tired kings pay well for a cup and a bed.
factory.wine.upgrade.name: Taverns
god.dagda.description: Call the Dagda of Ireland. His cauldron and harp fatten every wheel along the river.
god.dagda.name: Dagda
god.huangdi.description: Invoke the Yellow Emperor. The Mandate roots harvest and dynasty in your soil.
god.huangdi.name: Huangdi
god.indra.description: Stand beneath Indra of the Vedas. The thousand-eyed king sends bolts where arrows miss.
god.indra.name: Indra
god.inti.description: Bow to Inti of the Andes. Sun-temples blaze and pilgrim silver flows to your shrine.
god.inti.name: Inti
god.shango.description: Bind your forges to Shango, Yoruba thunder. Iron rings when the double axe falls.
god.shango.name: Shango
god.tangaroa.description: Sail with Tangaroa of Polynesia. The sea-father claims harbors and hulls for your crown.
god.tangaroa.name: Tangaroa

purchase.mode.max: Buy as many as you can
ui.about.realmDescription: Kingdoms don't fall because they ran out of gold. They fall because nobody fed the granary, sharpened the spears, or showed up to court. Start with one sad field of grain and build from there, even when you're off doing something else.
ui.about.title: About the Realm
ui.daily.description: Show up six days in a row. Free relics. You're welcome.
ui.daily.powerActive: You've already got a relic buzzing.
ui.daily.title: Daily reward
ui.factory.title: {0} workshop
ui.factoryGrid.label: Factories
ui.gods.description: Dump your gold now. The gods pay better interest later. (Probably.)
ui.gods.title: Gods
ui.install.description: Pin the realm to your home screen and pretend that's productivity.
ui.install.installedDescription: Idleval is already on this device.
ui.install.iosInstructions: On iPhone or iPad: tap Share, then Add to Home Screen.
ui.install.label: Install Idleval
ui.install.title: Install Idleval
ui.install.unavailableDescription: Chrome will light this up when the app can be installed here.
ui.inventory.description: Stack relics on the altar. Use them when the moment feels right, or panicked.
ui.inventory.title: Inventory
ui.managers.description: Legendary managers keep the wheels turning while you wander off.
ui.managers.title: Managers
ui.missions.description: Clear quests in order. Gold, relics, and bragging rights await.
ui.missions.imageAlt: Heracles, champion of the twelve labors
ui.missions.title: Quests
ui.nav.label: Game navigation
ui.offline.description: Your automated factories kept working while you were away. Come collect the loot.
ui.offline.imageAlt: Zurvan, Persian god of infinite time
ui.settings.imageAlt: Chiron, keeper of the realm's keys
ui.settings.subtitle: Volume, language, and the nuclear option.
ui.settings.title: Settings
ui.statistics.description: How much gold you've made, in numbers big enough to impress nobody.
ui.statistics.title: Statistics
ui.upgrades.description: Build the stuff that lasts and double what each pillar pays into the crown.
ui.upgrades.title: Upgrades
ui.welcome.disclaimer: Idleval is a fantasy parody. None of the gods, saints, managers, or cosmic accountants here are real, or meant to be.
ui.welcome.subtitle: Raise a tiny realm, hire strange helpers, court impossible powers, and watch your coffers grow while you are away.
ui.welcome.title: Welcome to Idleval
ui.wiki.description: Stories, gods, upgrades, and relics. The stuff that keeps a realm from starving or getting weird.
ui.wiki.imageAlt: Minerva, owl sage of the codex
ui.wiki.title: Royal Codex
wiki.factory.crossbow.manager.lore: William Tell split the apple on his son's head rather than bow to a tyrant's whim. Alpine guilds still drill by his example: steady breath, clean release, mark that never wavers.
wiki.factory.crossbow.manager.mechanics: Hire William for about 220× the base unit price. He taps for you from then on. Managers survive god resets. Only gold and factory counts wipe.
wiki.factory.crossbow.upgrade.lore: Trebuchets turned castle walls into negotiable rubble. Sometimes they hurled stones. Sometimes plague. Either way, the tower came down.
wiki.factory.crossbow.upgrade.mechanics: Costs about 1,000× the base unit price. Permanently doubles this factory's output. Upgrades stick through god invocations.
wiki.factory.grain.manager.lore: Demeter grieved for Persephone and once withered the whole earth. Eleusinian rites taught mortals that a full granary comes before good law.
wiki.factory.grain.manager.mechanics: Hire Demeter for about 220× the base unit price. She runs production while you wander off. Managers stay after a god reset.
wiki.factory.grain.upgrade.lore: Irrigation turned flood and drought into ledger entries. Cities could eat even when the rains forgot their schedule.
wiki.factory.grain.upgrade.mechanics: About 1,000× the base unit price. Doubles grain output forever. Survives god invocations.
wiki.factory.iron.manager.lore: Ilmarinen forged the Sampo and never let his anvil cool. In Idleval he shoes fields and armies alike, every ingot rings true.
wiki.factory.iron.manager.mechanics: Hire Ilmarinen for about 220× the base unit price. No more tapping this pillar. He keeps the job after you invoke a god.
wiki.factory.iron.upgrade.lore: Armories turned scrap into mail, buckles, ploughshares, and levy blades. Neighbors stopped being your only supplier.
wiki.factory.iron.upgrade.mechanics: About 1,000× the base unit price. Doubles iron output permanently. Still there after a god reset.
wiki.factory.longship.manager.lore: Sinbad crossed seven seas, outwitting roc, whale, and island-sized serpent. Every hull widens the crown past any single harbor.
wiki.factory.longship.manager.mechanics: Hire Sinbad for about 220× the base unit price. He sails the production loop for you. Managers persist through invocations.
wiki.factory.longship.upgrade.lore: Colonial trade sent companies across oceans, spice, wool, silver back; settlements on distant shores. Each voyage fattened map and purse.
wiki.factory.longship.upgrade.mechanics: About 1,000× the base unit price. Doubles longship output forever. Upgrades survive god resets.
wiki.factory.reliquary.manager.lore: Thomas Becket died in his own cathedral and became a saint whose shrine drew pilgrims from everywhere. Wax-sealed gifts still echo in every ledger.
wiki.factory.reliquary.manager.mechanics: Hire Becket for about 220× the base unit price. He handles the shrine while you scheme elsewhere. Managers stay after invocations.
wiki.factory.reliquary.upgrade.lore: Tithes turned faith into coin by law, every hearth paid, every child learned the creed, pilgrim silver filled the treasury.
wiki.factory.reliquary.upgrade.mechanics: About 1,000× the base unit price. Doubles reliquary output permanently. Keeps working after a god reset.
wiki.factory.wine.manager.lore: Siduri kept the tavern at the edge of the sea and told Gilgamesh to eat, drink, and rejoice instead of chasing immortality. Sound advice.
wiki.factory.wine.manager.mechanics: Hire Siduri for about 220× the base unit price. She pours while you don't tap. Managers survive god invocations.
wiki.factory.wine.upgrade.lore: The tavern league copied Siduri's garden, inns at crossroads and harbors where grape became coin through hospitality.
wiki.factory.wine.upgrade.mechanics: About 1,000× the base unit price. Doubles wine output forever. Upgrades persist through invocations.
wiki.god.dagda.lore: Irish father-god with a cauldron that never empties and a harp that orders seasons and sorrow. Warriors and farmers both wanted his nod.
wiki.god.dagda.mechanics: Costs 1 quintillion gold. Invoke for a permanent ×3 production boost. Resets gold and factory counts. God multipliers stack.
wiki.god.huangdi.lore: The Yellow Emperor founded civilization, medicine, writing, and the Mandate that ties harvest to dynasty. Law and grain, same soil.
wiki.god.huangdi.mechanics: Costs 1 trillion gold. Permanent ×2 production. Wipes gold and factories on invoke. Stacks with other gods.
wiki.god.indra.lore: Vedic storm king with a thousand eyes. Where arrows fail, his bolt still finds the mark.
wiki.god.indra.mechanics: Costs 1 nonillion gold. Permanent ×5 production. Resets run progress on invoke. Multipliers stack.
wiki.god.inti.lore: Inca sun god, father of the first emperor. Pilgrim silver flows to shrines that blaze when the valley lies in shadow.
wiki.god.inti.mechanics: Costs 1 tredecillion gold. Permanent ×10 production. Resets gold and factories. Stacks with other gods.
wiki.god.shango.lore: Yoruba orisha of thunder, once a mortal king whose double axe still cracks the sky. Smiths swear by him when the storm passes.
wiki.god.shango.mechanics: Costs 1 septillion gold. Permanent ×4 production. Resets on invoke. God bonuses multiply together.
wiki.god.tangaroa.lore: Polynesian sea-father who split ocean from sky. Sailors bargain for calm water, full nets, and a safe return to a wider crown.
wiki.god.tangaroa.mechanics: Costs 1 undecillion gold. Permanent ×8 production. Resets gold and factories. Stacks with other invoked gods.
wiki.powerup.hasteRune.lore: Carved on millstones and keels when a realm couldn't wait for wind, water, or tired hands. The glyph hurries the world, not the worker.
wiki.powerup.hasteRune.mechanics: Use from the altar. {duration}-minute buff: production runs at {timePercent}% speed. Only one timed relic at a time. From daily rewards and quests.
wiki.powerup.lightningShard.lore: Baltic amber sometimes traps a storm mid-strike, lightning frozen in honey glass. One shard can double a forge before thunder moves again.
wiki.powerup.lightningShard.mechanics: Use from the altar. {duration}-minute buff: each finished run pays double gold. One timed relic at a time. From dailies and quest rewards.
wiki.powerup.mimirCoin.lore: Mimir charges a coin for every glimpse from his well, gold you'd have earned if time had sprinted ahead. Bigger realm, heavier coin.
wiki.powerup.mimirCoin.mechanics: Instant from the altar: gold = your gold per second × a random roll. Rarity ranges: common {commonMin}–{commonMax} s, uncommon {uncommonMin}–{uncommonMax} s, rare {rareMin}–{rareMax} s, epic {epicMin}–{epicMax} s. Scales with factories, upgrades, managers, gods, and quest renown. From dailies and quests.
wiki.powerup.yggdrasilTear.lore: Sap from Yggdrasil's wounded bark is said to hurry every craft tied to the world-tree. A dawn tear can cram hours into one breath.
wiki.powerup.yggdrasilTear.mechanics: Instant from the altar: skips {duration} minutes ({advanceSeconds} s) on every running factory; finished runs still pay gold. Doesn't need a free buff slot. From dailies and quests.
